title: Support custom build outputs
body:
Docker has the ability to have custom build outputs, see https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/build/#custom-build-outputs. This is convenient when one wants to use a container as a build environment and then have the resulting executables conveniently copied to the host.

From research I have done, it seems like buildah and therefore podman are misssing this functionality, and I could not find any open tickets about this missing functionality in the tickets.

comments.body:
```
[](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13359#issue-1151676189)/kind feature

Description

I find the "local ouput" feature of buildx quite useful and use it extensively for my "short builds" (typically creating some .rpm/.deb packages of some of our internal tools already delivered as tarballs). I use a dockerfile with multi stage builds, where the very final target is "scratch" as base image, and I copy into this image the "result" of my build. When coupled with docker buildx --output type=local,dest=path feature, I can very easily get the result of my build in a local folder, without using things like "docker cp" or "podman mount". Plus, using "docker build" allows to benefit from docker caching so the same input instantely produces the same output when I have run it already (while I could use "docker run" in more complex builds like the ones I describe in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13358).

I understand you don't want to implement all the complexity of the buildx --ouput flag (actually, even on buildx side, this --output flag is half implemented as soon as you try doing multi-arch builds), but would it make sense to implement only the type=local output, or any equivalent podman flag ?
```
@joyfulmantis Thanks above PR should close this.

@rhatdan  I used the latest version of buildah and doesnt seem to create a tar.

```
[root@cf5f0621b1ae build]# buildah build --file alpine/Dockerfile -t alpine:latest -o type=tar,dest=/build/out.tar alpine/
```

@jpolara2016 Exact same command seems to work for me but my containerfile atleast had one RUN step , could you try adding extra `RUN echo hello` step to your `Dockerfile` to confirm.
```console
[root@fedora bin]# ./buildah build --file alpine/Dockerfile -t localhost/test -o type=tar,dest=/build/out.tar alpine/
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
STEP 2/2: RUN echo hello
hello
COMMIT localhost/test
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 24302eb7d908 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob f60e0a04f6ff done  
Copying config fd896f6ff1 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> fd896f6ff1f
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
fd896f6ff1f4490e14b24857f59cbf366b70aedd38a2d657a8456f709e914bfe
[root@fedora bin]# ls /build/
out.tar
[root@fedora bin]# ./buildah version
Version:         1.26.0

[root@fedora bin]#
```

Edit: feel free to open a new issue i think there is a missed edge-case of empty Containerfile with single `FROM <image` statement somehow shorts the flow of output generation add extra dummy STEP as workaround e.g `RUN echo hello` and feel free to open a new issue for this but I'll fix this anyways in upstream,

@rhatdan If I add one RUN step, it works. Thanks for checking!

Opened a new issue https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4094
